req.title: Submit timesheet — variant 3
req.statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a weekly timesheet submission with per-day hours in [0,24] and weekly total in [0,80], plus a mandatory webhook emission. I identified four partitions: (1) valid mid-range hours, (2) per-day hours outside [0,24], (3) weekly total outside [0,80], and (4) exact boundary values (0, 24, 80). State transition: draft -> submitted. DT covers the combined per-day AND weekly-total guard. I also verify the webhook event fires on success. Tests are self-contained via fixtures and helpers declared as placeholders.

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_valid_midrange_emits_webhook
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
Webhook listener is configured and reachable
actions: Build payload with 7 days each having 8 hours (total=56)
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
Capture HTTP response
Poll webhook listener for emitted event
expected: Webhook listener receives exactly one event with type=timesheet.submitted
trace: R-WEB-069: webhook event emission on successful submit

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_per_day_hours_exceeds_24_returns_422
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
actions: Build payload where Monday has 25 hours (outside [0,24])
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
Capture HTTP response
expected: Response body references per-day hours validation error
trace: R-WEB-069: error pathway — per-day hours outside 0-24 yields 422
R-WEB-069: negative partition for day hours > 24
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_submit_timesheet_per_day_hours_exceeds_24_returns_422(
    auth_token, api_client, draft_timesheet
):
    days = [{"day": d, "hours": 8} for d in ["tue", "wed", "thu", "fri", "sat", "sun"]]
    days.insert(0, {"day": "mon", "hours": 25})
    payload = {"timesheet_id": draft_timesheet["id"], "days": days}
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/timesheets/submit",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, resp.text
    error_body = resp.json()
    assert "hours" in str(error_body).lower() or "day" in str(error_body).lower()
    state_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/timesheets/{draft_timesheet['id']}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"},
    )
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "draft"

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_weekly_total_exceeds_80_returns_422
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
actions: Build payload where each day has valid hours but total=84 (>80)
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
Capture HTTP response
expected: Response body references weekly total validation error
trace: R-WEB-069: error pathway — weekly total outside 0-80 yields 422
R-WEB-069: DT row: per-day valid AND weekly total invalid => 422

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_boundary_max_day_and_max_week
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
actions: Build payload: Mon-Thu = 20 hours each (total=80), all remaining days = 0
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
Capture HTTP response
expected: No validation error for boundary-exact values
trace: R-WEB-069: BVA upper boundary — weekly total exactly 80
R-WEB-069: BVA valid per-day boundary — day hours exactly 20 (within [0,24])

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_boundary_zero_hours_all_days
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
actions: Build payload where all 7 days have 0 hours (lower boundary, total=0)
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
Capture HTTP response
expected: Zero-hour timesheet is accepted at lower boundary
trace: R-WEB-069: BVA lower boundary — per-day hours exactly 0
R-WEB-069: BVA lower boundary — weekly total exactly 0

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_state_transition_draft_to_submitted
preconditions: A timesheet exists in state=draft for the current week
actions: Verify timesheet is in draft state via GET /timesheets/{id}
POST valid payload to /timesheets/submit
Re-fetch timesheet state via GET /timesheets/{id}
expected: Final state is submitted (draft -> submitted transition)
trace: R-WEB-069: STC — timesheet transitions from draft to submitted on valid submit

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_negative_hours_returns_422
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
actions: Build payload where Wednesday has -1 hours (below lower boundary 0)
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
Capture HTTP response
expected: Response body references per-day hours validation error
trace: R-WEB-069: error pathway — per-day hours outside 0-24 (below 0) yields 422
R-WEB-069: NEG partition for day hours < 0
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_submit_timesheet_negative_hours_returns_422(
    auth_token, api_client, draft_timesheet
):
    days = [{"day": d, "hours": 8} for d in ["mon", "tue", "thu", "fri", "sat", "sun"]]
    days.insert(2, {"day": "wed", "hours": -1})
    payload = {"timesheet_id": draft_timesheet["id"], "days": days}
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/timesheets/submit",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, resp.text
    error_body = resp.json()
    assert "hours" in str(error_body).lower() or "day" in str(error_body).lower()
    state_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/timesheets/{draft_timesheet['id']}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"},
    )
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "draft"
